tracks.meaning: to separate or break something into two or more parts
to perform the arithmetic operation of splitting a number into equal parts
to cause disagreement or create a split in opinion or loyalty between parties
a ridge or watershed that separates water drainage into different basins or river systems
to distribute property, assets, or inheritance among multiple parties or beneficiaries
to reproduce by splitting into two separate organisms, as in cell division
to split data or a dataset into separate groups or partitions for processing
to separate a region or political entity into distinct administrative or political units
to share something equally or split the cost between people
to move apart or cause people or things to separate in different directions
to cause a disagreement or rift between people by creating conflicting viewpoints
